text: Mɔɔ menli ɛhye mɔ kpondɛ na bɛadɛnla yɛ avinli bɛ nee yɛ ayɛ ko la a le kɛ, yɛ mrenya ne mɔ, ko biala kɛyɛ ye mrenyanu nzonlɛ, kɛmɔ bɛyɛ bɛ ɛdeɛ ne la.
translated: I have seen that when you teach children good things and they refuse to do them, you leave them, and whatever they do is their own responsibility, because they have done it with their own hands.

ref: Three days later the men who had been circumcised were still weak from pain. So Simeon and Levi, two of Dinah's brothers, attacked with their swords and killed every man in the town,
text: Ye kenle nsa ne azo mɔɔ ɛnee bɛ nganlɛ ne mɔ ɛlɛfofo bɛ la, Gyekɔbo amra ne mɔ anu nwiɔ, Semɛyɔn nee Livae, Daena amediema mrenya ne mɔ, vale bɛ dadeɛ na bɛhɔle bɛ nwo zo wɔ suakpole ne azo arɛlevilɛ nu, na bɛhunle mrenya mɔɔ wɔ sua ne azo la amuala.
translated: When they heard that she had been defiled and that her brother had done this shameful thing to her, Dinah's brothers Simeon and Levi, two of Jacob's sons, took their swords and attacked the unsuspecting city, and killed all the males.

ref: While they were there, Rebekah's personal servant Deborah died. They buried her under an oak tree and called it “Weeping Oak.” God Blesses Jacob at Bethel
text: Ɛkɛ ne a Dɛbola, mɔɔ nea Relɛbɛka la wule a. Bɛziele ye wɔ oku baka bie abo wɔ Bɛtɛle nyiakɛ, na bɛvɛlɛle ɛkɛ ne “Ɛzunlɛ oku Baka.”
translated: It was Deborah, Rebekah's nurse. They buried her under an oak tree below Bethel, and they called it "The Oak of Weeping."

ref: Rachel was buried beside the road to Ephrath, which is also called Bethlehem.
text: Yemɔti Relahyɛle wule na bɛziele ye wɔ Yifelate adevinli ne. Ɛkɛ ne a ɛnɛ bɛfɛlɛ ye Bɛtelɛhɛm la.
translated: We have heard that it was found in Ephrathah, we found it in the fields of Jaar. Let us go to his dwelling place, let us worship at his footstool in Bethlehem.

ref: He married three Canaanite women: The first was Adah, the daughter of Elon the Hittite; the second was Oholibamah, the daughter of Anah and the granddaughter of Zibeon the Hivite;
text: Yisɔ gyale Keena mraalɛ. Bɛmɛ a le Hɛtenli Yilɔn ara Eda nee Enaa ara Ɔhɔlebama. Enaa ɔ ze a le Haevaenli Zebɛyɔn,
translated: These are the sons of Keena's wife. She bore to Heten Yilon and Eda and Enaa and Oholebama. Enaa's mother was Haeveanli Zebeyon,

ref: Lotan's sons were Hori and Heman; his sister was Timna.
text: Lotan abo zo amra a le Hɔlae nee Himan mbusua ne mɔ anu amra.
translated: Lot's two daughters had sexual relations with their father and both became pregnant.

ref: Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan, and they lived in the land of Seir.
text: Hɔlae mbusua ekpunli ekpunli mɔɔ wɔ Yidɔm azɛlɛ zo la mgbanyinli a le Lotan, Hyobale, Zebɛyɔn, Enaa, Daehyɔn, Yizɛ, yɛɛ Daehyan. Yidɔm Arelemgbunli (
translated: These are the clans that came from Seir in the land of the Horites who were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah, Dishon, Ezer, and Dishan. The Horite chiefs (

ref: Joseph said, “Let me tell you about my dream.
text: Ɔhanle ɔhilele bɛ kɛ, “Bɛdie ɛlalɛ bie mɔɔ meha la:
translated: The chief said to them, "They have eaten all my food:

ref: One day when Joseph's brothers had taken the sheep to a pasture near Shechem,
text: Kenle ko Dwosefi amediema ne mɔ vale bɛ ze mboane ne mɔ hɔle adidi wɔ Hyikɛm.
translated: So Joseph took his mother and went to live with his father-in-law in Egypt.

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.
text: Gyekɔbo zele Dwosefi kɛ, “Ɛ mediema ne mɔ ɛva mboane ne mɔ ɛhɔ adidi wɔ Hyikɛm. Bɛla maa menzoa wɔ bɛ nwo lɔ.” Dwosefi buale kɛ, “Egya, mewɔ ɛke, soa me.”
translated: Jacob said to Joseph, "Your brothers are grazing the flock at Shechem. Come, I will send you to them." Joseph answered, "Father, I am ready, send me."

ref: They said to one another, “Look, here comes the hero of those dreams!
text: Bɛhanle bɛhilele bɛ nwo ngoko kɛ, “Ahenle ne mɔɔ ka ɛlalɛ ne la a ɛlɛba la.
translated: They answered and said to him, "Our father is Abraham."

ref: As Joseph's brothers sat down to eat, they looked up and saw a caravan of Ishmaelites coming from Gilead. Their camels were loaded with all kinds of spices that they were taking to Egypt.
text: Akee bɛdɛnlanle aze kɛ bɛdi debie; na bɛmanle bɛ nye zo la, bɛnwunle kɛ Yehyemeeyɛle adendulɛma ekpunli bie ɛlɛba. Bɛvi Geleade, bɛlɛ bɛ afunlumu mɔɔ zozo ɛyɛlɛ, alufuanlɛ, yɛɛ ayile ndɔkɛ bɛlɛkɔ Yigyibiti.
translated: And the Ishmaelites came that they were eating food; and when they saw him, they sold him to the Ishmaelites for twenty pieces of silver. They took him to Gilead, they and their camels carrying spices, balm, and myrrh going down to Egypt.

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Na Gyekɔbo suosuanle ye tɛladeɛ ne mɔɔ ɔwula la anu, ɔvale ɛrele ɔpɛle ɔ bo, na ɔzunle ɔ ra ne mekɛ dɔɔnwo.
translated: And when the Tortoise saw that there was no way out, he pretended to be dead, and lay down without moving.

ref: The third one was born when Judah was in Chezib, and she named him Shelah.
text: Eza ɔwole nrenya bieko, na ɔdonle ye Hyila. Dwuda wɔ Akezebe la yɛɛ ɔwole ralɛ nrenya ɛhye a.
translated: A woman had two children, and she loved Hyila. When they were at Akezebe, the woman was taking care of the children.

ref: Later, Judah chose Tamar as a wife for Er, his oldest son.
text: Na Dwuda gyale raalɛ maanle ɔ ra kpanyinli Ɛɛ. Bɛfɛlɛ raalɛ ne Teemaa.
translated: And David took more concubines and wives out of Jerusalem. They bore him Tema.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.
text: Na Dwuda ara nrenya kpanyinli Ɛɛ le amumuyɛ, yemɔti Ɛdɛnkɛma hunle ye.
translated: And David did not take the number of those from twenty years old and under, because the LORD had said he would multiply Israel like the stars of the heavens.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Mekɛ guale nu la Hyua ara raalɛ ne mɔɔ Dwuda gya ye la wule. Ɔwiele ye kunlane ne kyi la, ɔhɔle ɛleka mɔɔ bɛpepɛ ye mboane nwo enrinli la wɔ Temenasuazo. Ɔkɔ la ɔ nee ɔ gɔnwo Haela mɔɔ vi Adɔlam la a hɔle a.
translated: I will go down to Judah and stay with my friend David for a while. He will welcome me and give me a place to stay, and I will find rest from all the troubles in Temansuazo. When he goes, he and his friend Haela will meet in Adullam and stay there.

ref: As Tamar was being dragged off, she sent someone to tell her father-in-law, “The man who gave me this ring, this cord, and this walking stick is the one who got me pregnant.”
text: Mɔɔ bɛtwili ye bɛahɔyela ye la, ɔvale nrɛlaleɛ ɔhɔmanle ɔ hu ɔ ze Dwuda kɛ, “Ahenle mɔɔ lɛ ninyɛne ɛhye mɔ la a nee me ɛlɛnrenzɛ a. Yemɔti mebodo wɔ, nea bɛ kpalɛ na ɛbanwu ahenle mɔɔ kpɔdelera ne, nyɛma ne, yɛɛ kpɔma ne le ye ɛdeɛ la.”
translated: When they had finished celebrating, the prophet who had come with David said to him, "The king should not think that he has done me a favor. I know my place, and they will not forget the king's kindness, his goodness, and his love for them."

ref: The Ishmaelites took Joseph to Egypt and sold him to Potiphar, the king's official in charge of the palace guard.
text: Yehyemeeyɛlema dɔle Dwosefi la, bɛ nee ye hɔle Yigyibiti, na Yigyibitinli bie mɔɔ bɛfɛlɛ ye Pɔtefaa, mɔɔ le Yigyibiti belemgbunli mgbanyima ne mɔ ko, na eza ɔle suakunlu ɛkɛ ne sinzavolɛma safohyenle la, dɔle Dwosefi vile bɛ ɛkɛ.
translated: When they took Joseph to Egypt, an Egyptian named Potiphar, who was one of Pharaoh's officials and captain of the guard, bought him from the Ishmaelites who had taken him there.

ref: So Joseph lived in the home of Potiphar, his Egyptian owner. Soon Potiphar realized that the Lord was helping Joseph to be successful in whatever he did.
text: na ɔ menle ne nwunle kɛ Ɛdɛnkɛma li Dwosefi anzi, na ɔmanle debie biala mɔɔ ɔvale ɔ sa ɔhanle ye la yɛle kpalɛ.
translated: and he did not know her until she had given birth to a son, and he did not know any woman until he married her and they lived together.

ref: No one in my master's house is more important than I am. The only thing he hasn't given me is you, and that's because you are his wife. I won't sin against God by doing such a terrible thing as this.”
text: Melɛ tumi wɔ awuke ye anu kɛ ɔdaye ala la, yɛɛ ɔtɛtuale me kɛ memmafa me sa meka debie biala kɛ ɛdawɔ ala, ɔboalekɛ ɔ ye a le wɔ. Duzu ati yɛɛ akee menwɔyɛ amumuyɛ kpole ɛhye, na menwɔyɛ ɛtane menwɔtia Nyamenle ɛ?”
translated: I have the power to lay down my life and to take it up again, because this is the command I received from my Father. Therefore, if I do not do the works of my Father, do not believe me, but if I do them, even though you do not believe me, believe the works?

ref: The jailer did not worry about anything, because the Lord was with Joseph and made him successful in all that he did.
text: Yeanvane nrenya ne mɔɔ nea pilizama anwo zo la anwo kɛ ɔkɛnlea debie mɔɔ bɛvale bɛwulale Dwosefi asa anu la, ɔboalekɛ ɛnee Ɛdɛnkɛma nee ye a lua a, yɛɛ debie biala mɔɔ ɔyɛle la, Ɛdɛnkɛma manle ɔyɛle kpalɛ.
translated: When they saw that the Pharisees and those who were with them wanted to kill Joseph, because he was not doing what they wanted him to do, and whatever they did, God did not allow it to happen.

ref: and in three days the king will pardon you. He will make you his personal servant again, and you will serve him his wine, just as you used to do.
text: Ɛnɛ anwo zo kenle nsa, belemgbunli ne bamaa bɛaraye wɔ wɔ efiade ɛke na bɛava wɔ gyima ne bɛawula ɛ sa nu; ɛbava belemgbunli ne kɔpo ne wɔawula ɔ sa nu, na wɔazu ye nza kɛ dɛba ne ala la.
translated: And these days, the youth and their parents are not careful about the things they will do in the future and how they will end; therefore the youth are careless about how they end up, and they become useless in society.

ref: But when these good things happen, please don't forget to tell the king about me, so I can get out of this place.
text: Na saa ɔyɛ boɛ ɔmaa wɔ a, anrɛɛ kakye me na yɛ boɛ ɛhye maa me. Mebodo wɔ, bɔ me duma kile belemgbunli ne maa ɔye me ɔvi efiade ɛke.
translated: When she does something for them, instead of thanking me, they do something that hurts me. I am tired of it, so I have decided to stop and let her be my only child.

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Ɔluakɛ nɔhalɛ nu, bɛwuale me a bɛ nee me vi Hibulu azɛlɛ zo rale a, yɛɛ ɛke noko, metɛyɛle ɛhwee mɔɔ ɔwɔ kɛ bɛto me efiade a.”
translated: When the time came, they brought me and my Hebrew friend out together, and indeed, I saw that everything happened just as he had told me."

ref: The top basket was full of all kinds of baked things for the king, but birds were eating them.”
text: Mɔɔ gyi zolɛ kpalɛ anwuma la, aleɛ ngakyile dɔɔnwo mɔɔ bɛdo ye fonlomo nu la wɔ nu mɔɔ mefa meahɔmaa belemgbunli ne a, na ɛnee nloma ɛlɛsuso ye me ti zo ali.”
translated: "I have worked hard, but the money I have saved is not enough for what I want to do with my life, so I need more help."

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Ɛvolɛ nwiɔ anzi, Yigyibiti belemgbunli ne hanle ɛlalɛ kɛ ɛnee ɔgyi Azule Naele ne anloa.
translated: In ancient times, the Egyptians used to worship and serve the god of the Nile River.

ref: Suddenly, seven fat, healthy cows came up from the river and started eating grass along the bank.
text: Ɔnwunle kɛ nlankɛ mgbole, dɔlera nsuu vi azule ne anu ɛvinde na bɛlɛdidi ɛhonle ne mɔɔ wɔ ɛkɛ ne la anu.
translated: He/She was not feeling well, so he/she drank some water to swallow the medicine that would help him/her get better and feel good in his/her body.

ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.
text: Yemɔti aleɛ hyenle la ɛnee ye adwenle ɛzɛkye. Na ɔzoanle ɔmanle bɛhɔvɛlɛle ngonyiama mɔɔ wɔ Yigyibiti la amuala nee maanle ne anu nrɛlɛbɛvolɛma ne amuala na ɔhanle ye ɛlalɛ ne ɔhilele bɛ. Noko bɛ nuhua ko bɔbɔ angola ɛlalɛ ne abo kile kile belemgbunli ne.
translated: We should think carefully about this matter. And the people of Israel were oppressed by Pharaoh in Egypt with hard labor and bitter suffering with hard labor and they cried out to God. But God heard their cry and delivered them with signs and great wonders.

ref: A young Hebrew, who was a servant of the captain of the guard, was there with us at the time. When we told him our dreams, he explained what each of them meant,
text: Mekɛ ne anu ɛnee yɛ nee Hibulunli kpavolɛ bie mɔɔ le sinzavolɛma safohyenle ne sonvolɛ la a gua ɛkɛ a. Yɛhanle yɛ ɛlalɛ ne mɔ yɛhilele ye, na ɔhilele ɛlalɛ ne mɔ abo ɔhilele yɛ, kɛmɔ sonla ko biala ye ɛlalɛ ne de la.
translated: I have done this so that the Hebrews may know that I am greater than their gods and their idols. What they worship and I worship are not the same, because they worship what they worship, but idolatry is not in my worship.

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
text: Dwosefi buale kɛ, “Nana, medame me ngome mengola. Nyamenle yɛɛ bahile ɔ bo ahile Nana a.”
translated: Joseph answered and said, "Master, I have finished my work. God has helped me to complete it, Master."

ref: The skinny cows ate the fat ones.
text: Na nlankɛ nwanlera, kengamgbaanwo nsuu ne hyele alumua nlankɛ mgbole dɔlera nsuu ne lile.
translated: And in the morning, the children went to the river and fetched water in the morning and came back home.

ref: But you couldn't tell it, because these skinny cows were just as skinny as they were before. At once, I woke up.
text: Noko bɛlile bɛ bɛwiele bɔbɔ la, ɛnee ɛnwu nlankɛ ne mɔ a ɛkɛze kɛ bɛtɛdile ɛhwee, ɔluakɛ ɛnee kɛ bɛde ala la yɛɛ bɛde a. Na mendunwuele.
translated: When they said they would kill him, he didn't know what to do because they had tied him up, so he just stayed there. That's my story.

ref: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
text: Na Dwosefi buale kɛ, “Nana, ɛlalɛ ne le kokye. Nyamenle ɛye mɔɔ ɔbayɛ la ɛhile Nana.
translated: And Joseph answered saying, "Master, it is not in my power. God is the one who will give an answer to Master."

ref: For seven years Egypt will have more than enough grain,
text: Ɛvolɛ nsuu bara mɔɔ aleɛ babu kpole wɔ Yigyibiti azɛlɛ ye azo a.
translated: The river did not overflow its banks in Egypt this year.

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: Ɛlalɛ fane nwiɔ ne mɔɔ Nana hanle la kile kɛ Nyamenle ɛbɔ ye kpɔkɛ ɛwie, na ɔnrɛhyɛ biala na yeazi.
translated: Therefore whoever hears these words of mine and does them, he will be like a wise man who built his house on the rock.

ref: Then appoint some other officials to collect one fifth of every crop harvested in Egypt during the seven years when there is plenty.
text: Nana ɛbɔ ɔ bo ɛyeye menli mɔɔ banlea azɛlɛ ne mɔ azo la, na ɔlie aleɛ biala mɔɔ kɛvinde Yigyibiti azɛlɛ ye azo la anu ngyɛnu nnu ɛkpɔ ko wɔ ɛvolɛ nsuu ne mɔɔ aleɛ babu la anu.
translated: God will do the same wonders for us today as He did when He brought the Israelites out of Egypt and dried up the Red Sea for them to cross.

ref: So the king said to them, “Who could possibly handle this better than Joseph? After all, the Spirit of God is with him.”
text: Na ɔzele ye mgbanyima ne mɔ kɛ, “Bɛnea a awie bie wɔ ɛkɛ tɛla ɛhye mɔɔ Nyamenle Sunsum ne wɔ ye nu la ɔ?”
translated: "And the angel answered and said to her, 'The Holy Spirit will come upon you and the power of the Most High will overshadow you.'"
